name: HCQS / Plaquenil
source_note: Data compiled for Arogya knowledge base. Verify against 1mg.com and pharmeasy.in for live updates.
how_it_works: Accumulates in lysosomes and raises intralysosomal pH, impairing antigen processing and pro-inflammatory cytokine production. Also has antimalarial activity.

serious: Macular retinopathy and irreversible vision loss (dose-and duration-dependent)

food_interactions: Take with food or milk to reduce GI upset
what_to_avoid: Annual ophthalmology exam after 5 years of use
Takes months to show full effect in autoimmune disease
